title: Intrinsic absorption spectroscopy and related physical quantities of narrow-gap semiconductors Hg1-xCdxTe
notes: Primary abstract confirms thin films made from bulk material; fitted absorption curves; Burstein-Moss shift, Urbach tail, and intrinsic concentration discussed. Point table and sample metrology not recovered.

title: Band-to-band optical absorption in narrow-gap Hg1-xCdxTe semiconductors
notes: Primary abstract defines Eg at the transition between the Urbach exponential region and the Kane region; samples thinner than 10 um. Composition range exactly matches the secondary Chu-Sher Table 4.4 screen, but the primary point table is not recovered.

title: Energy Gap in Hg1-xCdxTe by Optical Absorption
notes: Primary abstract and empirical formula recovered. Point-level x-Eg-T values, composition method, and uncertainties not recovered.

title: Temperature and Alloy Compositional Dependences of the Energy Gap of Hg1-xCdxTe
notes: Primary abstract and empirical formula recovered. Detector spectral-response curves and point-level source data are not recovered.

title: Energy gap versus alloy composition and temperature in Hg1-xCdxTe
notes: Primary metadata states that 22 studies were combined and reports a 0.013 eV standard error. The 22-study point ledger, per-source definitions, and weights are not recovered.

title: Energy gap versus alloy composition and temperature in Hg1-xCdxTe
notes: Primary formula and declared validity range recovered. Point-level optical data and composition provenance not recovered.

title: New nonlinear temperature dependence of the HgCdTe energy gap
notes: Primary PDF and specimen-level digitized temperature series are present in the repository. Only one temperature-series specimen has independently tied composition; shape tests profile specimen offsets.

title: Observation of three-dimensional massless Kane fermions in a zinc-blende crystal
notes: Primary full text reports a roughly 3.2 um graded plateau near x approximately 0.17 controlled by in-situ single-wavelength ellipsometry. A 4 meV gap improves the low-field model, with EF approximately 15-17 meV and n approximately (2-3)e14 cm^-3. The record is carrier-coupled, composition-censored, and shares the Mikhailov/Dvoretskii source lineage with Teppe; it is not an exact homogeneous composition-gap point or independent cross-laboratory validation.

title: Physics and Properties of Narrow Gap Semiconductors Table 4.4
notes: Eight values are available as a secondary transcription and are attributed to Chu-Mi-Tang experiments. They are external screen evidence, not primary fit authority.
